title: WebSockets
description: Managed WebSocket endpoints with shared middleware and optional port isolation

blocks.text: app.ws(path, handlers, options?) registers a WebSocket endpoint that runs through the same middleware pipeline as the rest of the app — auth, request id, logging, and ctx.state populated by your JWT/cookie middleware all work unchanged.

API matches Fresh 2.3's app.ws() (PR #3774): always managed mode, handlers receive the upgraded WebSocket plus the request Context, returns this for chaining. Non-WebSocket requests to the path automatically return 426 Upgrade Required.
By default, WS routes share the main HTTP port. The handler runs after global middleware, so ctx.state is populated by your auth pipeline before the upgrade fires.
Don't re-parse cookies in the open handler. Your existing JWT/cookie middleware already runs for every WS upgrade — just read ctx.state.userContext like any other route.

Set options.port to bind the endpoint to its own Deno.serve listener. Howl spawns the secondary listener automatically when app.listen() runs. The same global middleware pipeline executes (auth, ctx.state work identically), but the WS path is hidden from the main listener — only the WS routes registered on that port are reachable on it.

Useful when you want to scale the WS surface independently or terminate TLS separately. Multiple WS routes can share a port — call app.ws() again with the same port and they're added to the same listener.

rows: Other routes on the WS port
Idle timeout in seconds — forwarded to Deno.upgradeWebSocket
Bind to a separate Deno.serve listener (Howl extension)
Hostname for the secondary listener (defaults to main app's hostname)
